decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing artifact cards to create a prison-like control over the game, restricting the opponent's options and ultimately locking them out of playing effectively. By utilizing powerful artifacts such as Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms, along with disruptive cards like Trinisphere and Pithing Needle, the deck aims to create a dominant board presence while hindering the opponent's ability to play their own strategies. The key concept is to establish control through artifacts and lock down the opponent's options until victory is achieved.

This Aluren combo deck aims to quickly assemble the pieces needed to cheat out powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Acererak the Archlich using cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience. Utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Ponder for card selection, along with Force of Will and Veil of Summer for protection, the deck aims to win the game by overwhelming the opponent with high-value creatures and utilizing Stock Up to draw more cards. The deck also includes mana acceleration with cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb to quickly power out its combo pieces.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to create a prison-like control over the opponent, locking down their resources and preventing them from executing their game plan effectively. By utilizing powerful artifacts such as Coveted Jewel, Paradox Engine, and The One Ring, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Trinisphere and Relic of Sauron. Additionally, with access to powerful finishers like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, the deck is capable of closing out games quickly once it has taken control of the battlefield.

The deck focuses on using the "Show and Tell" card to quickly drop powerful creatures like Emrakul onto the battlefield. It includes cards like Sneak Attack and Omniscience to cheat these creatures into play for an explosive win. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Will and protection spells like Veil of Summer to protect its combo pieces. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive creatures and win the game through a quick and powerful combo. The deck is designed to be efficient and deadly in Legacy 1 vs 1 matchups.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed for a win condition using cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Doomsday. The goal is to use Doomsday to strategically stack the top cards of the library, followed by casting Thassa's Oracle to win the game. The deck includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo, along with card selection and manipulation tools like Brainstorm and Ponder to set up the combo. The deck aims for a fast, efficient win through careful planning and execution.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through the use of lands and various land-based abilities. By utilizing cards like Life from the Loam, Wasteland, and Crop Rotation, the player can disrupt their opponent's mana base and establish a strong board presence. The deck also includes powerful cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to quickly create a formidable threat. With cards like Sphere of Resistance and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while steadily building towards a win condition.

This deck aims to quickly and efficiently play as many spells as possible in order to combo off and win the game in a single turn. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Simian Spirit Guide, the deck is able to generate a large amount of mana to cast a chain of spells that will allow the player to empty their library and win with Thassa's Oracle. The deck also includes tools for disruption such as Cabal Therapy and Pact of Negation to protect the combo and ensure it goes off smoothly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty, Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Griselbrand using spells such as Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave. The deck includes disruptive spells like Unmask, Thoughtseize, and Cabal Therapy to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The mana base is designed to support these plays with fetchlands like Polluted Delta and Bloodstained Mire, as well as fast mana like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual. The goal is to bring out powerful threats early and overwhelm the opponent before they can react.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana and cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with these powerful creatures and disrupt their game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce. The deck also includes card draw and selection tools like Brainstorm and Ponder to ensure consistency in drawing the combo pieces needed to win the game efficiently.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a lock-down on the opponent's resources by utilizing artifacts that disrupt their gameplay, such as Trinisphere and Pithing Needle. The deck also aims to generate mana quickly with cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's Tower in order to play powerful threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. Additionally, the deck includes key artifacts like The One Ring and Planar Nexus to provide additional control and win conditions.

The deck revolves around assembling powerful artifact combos, using cards like The One Ring and Coveted Jewel to generate resources and control the board. It includes strong ramp options like Grim Monolith and Ancient Tomb to accelerate into game-winning plays like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. It also features disruptive elements such as Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to interact with the opponent's game plan. With access to efficient tutors like Transmute Artifact and Stock Up, the deck aims to quickly set up its win condition and overwhelm the opponent with its versatile gameplay.

The deck focuses on using Gaea's Cradle to ramp up mana quickly and efficiently, while also utilizing control elements like Wasteland and Endurance to disrupt the opponent's strategy. By deploying a mix of creatures like Badgermole Cub and Springheart Nantuko, along with powerful spells like Natural Order and Green Sun's Zenith, the deck aims to establish board presence and eventually overwhelm the opponent with big threats like Craterhoof Behemoth. Additionally, the inclusion of utility creatures like Collector Ouphe and Bojuka Bog helps to disrupt opponent strategies and protect your own game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana to play powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The strategy revolves around using Show and Tell and Sneak Attack to cheat these creatures into play without paying their high mana costs. The combination of cards like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Force of Will allow for card selection and protection against disruption from the opponent. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats early in the game, securing a quick victory through a high-impact combo.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize. The deck's strategy revolves around playing powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls, while using efficient disruption with cards like Cabal Therapy. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive game plan with lands like Wasteland and Karakas. Overall, this deck aims to apply early pressure, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to establish a lockdown on the game board, creating a prison-like environment for the opponent. By ramping up mana quickly with cards like Grim Monolith and Ancient Tomb, the deck aims to play powerful control cards such as Trinisphere and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The synergy between cards like Urza's Saga, Karn, the Great Creator, and Candelabra of Tawnos allows for a versatile toolbox to handle various situations. Ultimately, the deck aims to control the game and pivot to victory with powerful finishers like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

This deck aims to control the game by utilizing the Nic Fit archetype, which focuses on generating value through sacrificing creatures and ramping into bigger threats. The key concept revolves around using cards like Veteran Explorer and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan while ramping into powerful creatures like Grist, the Hunger Tide and Balustrade Wurm. The deck also includes removal spells like Assassin's Trophy and Pernicious Deed to handle threats on the board. Overall, the goal is to outvalue the opponent and secure victory through attrition and powerful threats.
